tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and glycosuria with low serum glucose and HbA1c, suggesting possible acute or chronic renal dysfunction with impaired glucose handling. Urine is cloudy, brown, and has low specific gravity, further supporting renal impairment. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and assessment for possible acute kidney injury or underlying chronic kidney disease.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
